creator: matt@matthoran.com
text:
(In reply to Dmitri Goutnik from comment #5)
Well then I'm confused as this has been working for some time until the recent Go release. As mentioned I discussed with a Go team member and we discovered that this will happen if there is a .git directory at build time; Go will try to stamp the release but git isn't available.

The point in assigning this to you was that this is likely affecting packages globally; the build for lego hasn't changed in some time yet the stamping was just recently released.

Adding -buildvcs=false in my test did work as expected, but I believe this is a larger problem. The Go team member I contacted was happy to dig into this further with anyone who is interested.

Otherwise we can just add -buildvcs=false to this port to resolve.
